subject: UX & Research
title: The Future of Accessible AI
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Future of Accessible AI usable.

hook.text: AI describes images, transcribes speech, and generates captions in real time. Accessibility is automating.

buildup.text: Microsoft's Seeing AI describes the world to blind users: reading text and identifying people via phone camera.

discovery.text: Auto-generated alt text and live captions are becoming standard features in major platforms.

twist.text: AI alt text often misses context: 'a group of people' vs. 'a team celebrating a launch.' Nuance matters.

climax.text: AI automates the basics. Human judgment handles nuance. Together they scale accessibility further.

punchline.text: AI handles volume, humans handle meaning. Together they scale access.

quiz.question: What is the main limitation of AI-generated accessibility features?
options: AI misses context and nuance — it describes what it sees but not what it means
AI accessibility features are too expensive
AI only works with English content
